evidence: Direct bidirectional dependency exists between `src/domains/generation` and `src/shared`: `src/domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationAction.ts` imports `useProjectSelectionContext` from `src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx`, while `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx` imports `useToggleGenerationStar` from `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`.

`src/shared` depends broadly on domain types (`src/domains/generation/types/index.ts` is imported from many shared hooks/components), while domain hooks also depend on shared runtime/error/query infra (`src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`). This removes a stable dependency direction.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` introduces a global initialization gate (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient`) and hard runtime-order contract; `src/app/bootstrap.tsx` must call `initializeSupabase()`, while many non-integration modules call `getSupabaseClient()` directly.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx` carries a very wide `MediaLightboxProps` surface mixing navigation, editing, tasks, shot workflow, pane layout, and integration concerns, then re-bundles them in `useBundledLightboxProps`.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx` defines a large `LightboxSessionModel` with many nested contracts and extensive pass-through mapping, indicating orchestration-by-prop-plumbing rather than clear ownership boundaries.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` couples overlay event policy, viewport/pane layout integration, and accessibility framing in one shell, reinforcing mixed responsibility at the lightbox boundary.

Mechanical-signal rationale mentioned deferred import pressure and boundary violations; boundary drift is confirmed in imports above, while deferred import density data is absent in this packet and seed files show no notable function-level dynamic import concentration.

Prompt formatting logic is duplicated with different conventions: `submitSegmentTask.ts` `applyPromptAffixes()` joins with spaces, while `buildBatchTaskParams.ts` composes comma-delimited prompt strings inline.

The same prompt concept is represented with different naming/protocols across adjacent modules (`base_prompt`, `enhanced_prompt`, `fullPrompt`, `shortPrompt`) without a single boundary adapter, so convention drift is leaking into behavior.

Mechanical `duplicate_clusters` did not surface a direct duplicate cluster in these seed files, but local inspection confirms non-identical duplicated prompt-building logic that creates behavioral outliers.

Packet did not provide `holistic_context.errors.exception_hotspots`; only `errors.strategy_by_directory` is available, which still shows mixed strategies in relevant areas (`tasks/`: throws+returns_null+try_catch; `components/`: returns_null+try_catch+throws).

`submitSegmentTask.ts` mixes fail-open and fail-fast in one flow: metadata fetch/update failures are normalized and returned from (`saveEnhancedPromptMetadata`), while settings-save failures throw and abort submission (`maybeSaveSettings`).

`huggingFaceTokenVerifier.ts` swallows all thrown error detail in a broad catch and returns a generic failure string, while repository/task paths throw concrete errors (`paginatedTaskRepository.ts`, `toolSettingsWriteRepository.ts`).

Task seam is type-unsafe at boundary: `segmentSettingsUtils.buildTaskParams()` returns `Record<string, unknown>` and `submitSegmentTask.ts` force-casts to `IndividualTravelSegmentTaskParams` via `as unknown as`, hiding contract mismatches until runtime.

Queue/refetch workflow has loop-prone behavior: `useProcessingRefetchGuard.ts` repeatedly triggers `query.refetch()` whenever stale-empty conditions persist, with no retry cap/circuit breaker beyond time backoff in `taskFetchPolicy.ts`.
This creates integration friction at seams (task creation and task polling) where correctness depends on convention rather than explicit contracts and guardrails.

Lightbox flow is heavily layered: `MediaLightbox` bundles props, `ImageLightbox`/`VideoLightbox` remap again, `useSharedLightboxState` remaps into multiple sub-facades, then `LightboxLayout` consumes context slices. This creates high handoff volume with little behavior added at each layer.

`WorkflowControlsBarProps` carries a very wide API surface (many fields across shot selection, optimistic updates, navigation, and actions), and most of it is forwarded into child controls instead of being normalized once.

Mechanical abstraction signals are confirmed for indirection hotspots (`ImageLightbox`, `useVideoLightboxRenderModel`, `LightboxLayout`, `useSharedLightboxState`) and wide param bags in lightbox/task modules; this is not isolated to one file.

Type import boundary is fragmented and inconsistent: modules still import from legacy/non-canonical paths (`@/types/shots`, `../../../types/shots`) while the canonical surface is `@/domains/generation/types`.
`@/types/shots` currently has no backing file in `src/types`, so those imports are invalid path dependencies.

Mechanical concern about dep cycles was investigated in sampled hotspot files; no concrete cycle was confirmed in this batch scope. The stronger confirmed issue is dependency drift through stale compatibility paths.
Contract drift exists between generation row domain types and helpers: `PersistedGenerationRow` uses `createdAt`, while `variantToGenerationRow` writes `created_at` directly.

DTO aliasing is defined separately in `generationRowDto.ts`, but helper code writes transport-style keys into domain-facing `GenerationRow`, blurring boundary discipline and making mapping behavior ambiguous.

Mechanical migration signal (`mixed_extensions: ['index']`) is mostly refuted as a primary defect for this batch; index barrels here are largely intentional, but boundary key-shape drift is a concrete low-level quality issue.

`src/shared/lib/__tests__/timelineWriteQueue.test.ts` validates ordering and timeout helper behavior but does not cover a never-resolving `runSerializedTimelineWrite` task blocking all queued writes for a shot.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-core.test.ts` only asserts the current `contains('tasks', JSON.stringify([taskId]))` call shape and non-PGRST116 fallback-to-null behavior; it does not test duplicate-match / multi-row cases that can bypass de-duplication.

Several seed modules used as contract boundaries have no direct tests (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskIdParser.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/repositories/realtimeConnectionRepository.ts`, `src/features/layout/contracts/overlayViewportConstraints.ts`), while their behavior is depended on by higher-level flows.

`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts` standardizes request parsing via `OperationResult`, but `supabase/functions/complete_task/request.ts` introduces a separate `{ success: boolean }` parser/result protocol and custom `badRequest` mapping.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/request.ts` parses JSON with `req.json()` and returns only `invalid_json_body`; shared parser distinguishes body-shape failures (`invalid_json_body_shape`) and supports strict/loose modes.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskIdParser.ts` treats empty strings as status `ok`; downstream `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` propagates that `ok` status even when task IDs are semantically unusable.

Mechanical scan flagged extreme comment density in seed files (e.g., `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/TimelineContainer/index.tsx` comment_ratio 0.73, `ShotEditor/state/index.ts` 0.62, `ShotEditor/ui/index.ts` 0.62).
Several files are mostly narrative scaffolding/barrel commentary with little executable behavior, which increases drift risk and review noise without enforcing contracts.
The migration narrative in comments is stronger than runtime enforcement in adjacent code paths (e.g., legacy compatibility still actively embedded in structure-video state types and loaders).

Legacy deprecation markers are confirmed (11 deprecated fields in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`), but runtime/UI paths still consume and persist legacy-shaped fields.
`useStructureVideo.migrateToArrayFormat` forcibly writes `structure_type: 'uni3c'` for both already-array data and migrated single-video data, collapsing older structure variants during migration.

`StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` extends `LegacyStructureVideoFields` in UI state (`uiTypes.ts`), then is persisted back through `useStructureVideo` save path, keeping deprecated fields first-class instead of boundary-only.

Directory shape is overloaded at the root: `src/shared/components/` has 50 production files in `holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles`, and filesystem scan shows 61 top-level files at maxdepth=1.

`src/shared/hooks/` and `src/shared/lib/` are similarly flat (69 and 67 production files respectively in structure profile; 70 and 67 top-level files in filesystem scan), which weakens domain boundaries.

Coupling matrix confirms broad cross-layer fan-out from these flat roots (`src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/components/ui/` = 84, `src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/hooks/` = 41, `src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/lib/` = 22, `src/shared/hooks/ -> src/shared/lib/` = 51), indicating umbrella folders used as catch-alls.

Mechanical signal on large-file placement is confirmed in this batch scope: `src/shared/components/DatasetBrowserModal.tsx` is already flagged as structurally large and remains a top-level shared component despite mixed resource-domain behavior.

Packet `holistic_context.scan_evidence` does not expose `mutable_globals`/`errors.mutable_globals`; nearest available coupling signal is `systemic_patterns` with `window_global` (5 files), and code confirms mutable process-wide globals.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:11-23` gates all consumers behind mutable module state (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient`) and throws from `getSupabaseClient()` if bootstrap order is wrong.

`src/app/bootstrap.tsx:84-113` hard-codes the required init ordering (`initializeSupabase()` then optional debug global registration), creating implicit startup coupling for any module that calls Supabase client access early.

`src/integrations/supabase/debug/initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals.ts:29-30`, `src/integrations/supabase/reconnect/ReconnectScheduler.ts:147-161`, and `src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts:360-369` publish mutable singleton references onto `window`.

Confirmed concern: `WorkflowControlsBar` remains a bloated interface (31 props) and mostly forwards behavior to child controls, while `WorkflowControls` and `ShotSelectorControls` implement overlapping shot-association behavior.

Confirmed systemic pattern: lightbox architecture carries large contracts across layers (`MediaLightboxProps` flat mega-surface, `MediaGalleryLightbox` session decomposition/recomposition, no-op presenter wrappers in `imageLightboxModels.ts`).
High-complexity neighbors from scan evidence align with this pattern: `ImageLightbox.tsx` (981 LOC) and `useSharedLightboxState.ts` (819 LOC) centralize many responsibilities.
Refuted mechanical signal: `PhilosophyPane.tsx` no longer matches the old decomposition concern (now 161 LOC and delegates behavior to `usePhilosophyPaneMedia`/section components).
Refuted mechanical signal: `MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx` no longer shows the reported dead void-suppressed symbol pattern in current code.
Refuted/low-impact signals: `ProjectSelectorPopover` and `ReferenceThumbnail` signature-variance flags are cross-file naming/signature drift, not material local design defects in these files.
Naming across reviewed files is generally intent-revealing (`normalizeAndPresentError`, `resolveSettingsScopeTable`, `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm`).

Minor vocabulary drift remains where similar concepts use different terms (`settings`/`preferences`/`tool settings`) across `useToolSettings` and `useAutoTopup` paths, which adds small cognitive overhead.
naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: Terminology around persisted user/project configuration is not fully standardized across adjacent hooks and services.

evidence: `getStoredPromptCount` returns `parseInt(stored, 10)` directly; malformed session values produce `NaN` and no fallback to `1` (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/promptManagement/storedPromptCount.ts`).
That value is immediately fed into state initialization and persistence flow (`usePromptManagement`, `usePromptPersistenceEffects`), so downstream logic receives an invalid count without guardrails.

Mechanical dead-function signal for `fetchWithTimeout` is refuted: function contains full timeout/abort composition and multiple execution paths, not an empty or return-only body (`src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/fetchWithTimeout.ts`).
Most reviewed public contracts are explicitly typed and consistently consumed (`DeleteGenerationConfirmContract`, `SettingsScopeIdentifier`, lightbox prop contracts).
There are still soft type edges: multiple `Record<string, unknown>` patches/settings surfaces in settings and task builders reduce compile-time guarantees and rely on runtime assumptions.
type_safety.issues_preventing_higher_score: Broad `Record<string, unknown>` payload surfaces in settings/task pipelines leave important schema constraints unchecked at compile time.

evidence: `BatchImageGenerationTaskParams` and task creation contracts support `subject_reference_image` as an independent field (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`).

`buildBatchTaskParams` hardcodes `subject_reference_image` to `style_reference_image` and includes all reference fields only when `style_reference_image` is present, narrowing behavior relative to the broader shared contract (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/buildBatchTaskParams.ts`).
Form submission builds generic reference params (`buildReferenceParams`) but the batch builder rewrites part of that contract, creating a hidden behavior mismatch across modules.
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` fails closed when unauthenticated (`auth_required`) (`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts`).

In contrast, `useToolSettings` update mutation for `scope === 'user'` returns `null` when no user and treats it as successful no-op (`handleMutationSuccess` early-return), silently swallowing an auth failure path (`src/shared/hooks/useToolSettings.ts`).
This creates inconsistent auth semantics for the same settings subsystem: read path surfaces auth failure, write path can silently succeed without persisting.

findings.summary: Generation domain and shared layer are mutually dependent with no stable boundary direction
findings.suggestion: Introduce a one-way dependency rule: move cross-cutting generation contracts (IDs, row types, scope inputs) into a neutral `entities/generation` package, keep domain hooks depending only on domain/integration abstractions, and have shared UI call app-level adapters instead of domain hooks directly.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationAction` (domain) imports `useProjectSelectionContext` from shared context, binding domain actions to app/session context shape.
`MediaGallery/index.tsx` (shared UI) imports domain mutation hook `useToggleGenerationStar`, so shared also depends upward on domain behavior.
This two-way dependency pattern repeats with domain hooks using shared runtime/query/error modules and shared modules importing domain types/hooks, preventing enforceable module layering.

findings.summary: Global Supabase singleton leaks infrastructure dependency across feature and shared modules
findings.suggestion: Constrain `getSupabaseClient` usage to integration/repository modules and expose feature-facing repositories/services via dependency injection (context/provider or explicit factory) so runtime boot order is localized and testable.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` uses process-wide mutable state (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient`) and throws when accessed before bootstrap.
`src/app/bootstrap.tsx` performs mandatory global initialization (`initializeSupabase()`), creating hidden order dependence between bootstrap and consumers.
Many modules outside integration layer import `getSupabaseClient` directly, spreading infra concerns through pages, hooks, and shared services rather than containing them behind repositories.

findings.summary: Lightbox subsystem relies on oversized contract aggregation and pass-through orchestration
findings.suggestion: Split by responsibility: create mode-specific containers (`ImageLightboxContainer`, `VideoLightboxContainer`) with narrow inputs, move shared overlay mechanics into a reusable overlay boundary module, and reduce session model shape to minimal per-mode contracts.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` is a broad mixed interface (navigation, workflow, editing, task details, pane state, external integration), then re-grouped in `useBundledLightboxProps`, showing interface sprawl.
`MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx` defines `LightboxSessionModel` plus multiple sub-contract interfaces and mostly translates/destructures state into lightbox props, a high indirection handoff.
`LightboxShell.tsx` blends modal interaction policy with viewport/pane layout policy, making ownership boundaries between layout system and media workflow less direct.

findings.summary: Segment submission path inconsistently swallows vs throws errors across adjacent steps.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce per-layer error contracts: repositories return `Result` or throw consistently, UI orchestration maps those to user-facing outcomes in one place. For `submitSegmentTask`, decide whether metadata persistence is required (throw) or optional (explicitly mark best-effort and surface non-blocking warning). Preserve original caught error details in token verification and similar boundary utilities.
evidence: `saveEnhancedPromptMetadata()` logs/normalizes fetch/update failures then returns, allowing task creation to continue with stale metadata.
`maybeSaveSettings()` throws on false and aborts submission, so user-visible behavior changes by step rather than error class.
`verifyHuggingFaceToken()` uses a broad `catch {}` and drops root error context, unlike repository/task modules that throw upstream errors.

findings.summary: Task handoff relies on unsafe casts instead of an explicit typed boundary contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared exported DTO for segment task creation and make `buildTaskParams` return that DTO directly. Remove `unknown` cast chain and require compile-time satisfaction of required/optional fields before calling `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask`.
evidence: `buildTaskParams()` returns `Record<string, unknown>` while downstream API expects structured segment params.
`submitSegmentTask` converts that payload with `as unknown as IndividualTravelSegmentTaskParams`, bypassing compile-time validation.
The receiving path validates runtime fields (`validateIndividualTravelSegmentParams`) indicating compile-time seam is not trusted.

findings.summary: Processing refetch guard can churn indefinitely under persistent stale-empty states.
findings.suggestion: Add workflow guardrails: bounded retry counter, exponential cooldown, and a terminal 'degraded polling' state after N attempts. Persist guard state per filter key so reopening panes does not reset churn immediately, and emit a visible diagnostic state for manual recovery.
evidence: `useProcessingRefetchGuard` triggers `query.refetch()` whenever `shouldForceProcessingRefetch` is true and only tracks last timestamp, not attempts or terminal conditions.
`shouldForceProcessingRefetch` re-qualifies on stale empty success states with time-based thresholds, so long-lived backend gaps keep triggering periodic refetches.
No explicit guard exists for max retries, cooldown escalation, or operator-visible degraded state.

findings.summary: Prompt composition conventions diverge across generation paths, causing behavioral drift.
findings.suggestion: Extract one shared prompt assembly utility with explicit delimiter/normalization policy and use it in both segment and batch task builders. Keep legacy compatibility mode as a named option to avoid hidden drift.
evidence: `submitSegmentTask` uses `applyPromptAffixes` with space-join semantics.
`buildBatchTaskParams` builds prompt text inline with comma insertion logic and separate trimming behavior.
Both paths produce prompt payloads for task creation but lack a shared formatter contract, so edge-case punctuation/spacing differs by entry point.

findings.summary: Modules depend on non-existent or stale type shim paths instead of one canonical type boundary.
findings.suggestion: Create a single explicit compatibility shim (`src/types/shots.ts`) or remove legacy path usage entirely; then codemod all generation/shot imports to `@/domains/generation/types` and block old paths with a lint rule.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations.ts` imports `GenerationRow`/`Shot` from `@/types/shots`, but there is no `src/types/shots.ts` shim.
`VideoShotDisplay.tsx` imports from `../../../types/shots`, also pointing to a non-canonical/missing path pattern.
Canonical export surface already exists in `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts`, while legacy shims are split across `src/types/generationRow.ts` and `src/types/shot.ts`.

findings.summary: Two overlapping workflow-control components have drifted contracts and inconsistent behavior.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to one workflow-controls component (or one shared view-model hook + two thin renderers) with a single typed contract, then remove the duplicate prop surfaces.
evidence: Both `WorkflowControls` and `WorkflowControlsBar` model the same shot-association/apply-settings seam with large overlapping prop lists.
`WorkflowControls.tsx` passes `onNavigateToShot` into `useShotAssociationControls` despite not destructuring/binding it in component params, while `WorkflowControlsBar` wires navigation explicitly.
This is a concrete symptom of duplicated seam ownership: behavior and contracts are not evolving in one place.

findings.summary: Lightbox architecture uses excessive remapping facades that add indirection more than leverage.
findings.suggestion: Flatten the seam to one lightbox state contract per media type (image/video), move mapping to one boundary adapter, and pass only minimal render-ready models into layout/components.
evidence: `MediaLightbox` first repackages flat props into grouped objects, then downstream modules rebuild additional slices before rendering.

`useSharedLightboxState` exposes many sub-facades (`variants`, `navigation`, `shots`, `sourceGeneration`, `makeMainVariant`, `buttonGroupProps`) that are largely composition wrappers around other hooks.

`LightboxLayout` then consumes multiple context slices and still receives large prop bags (`workflowBar`, `buttonGroups`, segment/navigation/layout flags), indicating unresolved abstraction boundaries.

findings.summary: Cache update/invalidation logic is split across separate seams, increasing drift and maintenance risk.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single generation-cache policy module (shared by mutation hooks and invalidation hooks) that owns optimistic patch targets and invalidation/refetch strategy per operation.
evidence: `useToggleGenerationStar` performs manual optimistic edits across multiple query families (`unified`, `shots`, `generations.byShot`) and also dispatches app events.
Parallel invalidation helpers in `useGenerationInvalidation.ts` define separate key/invalidation policies for generation and variant changes.
Because mutation path and invalidation path both encode query topology, key changes require synchronized edits in multiple seams.

findings.summary: Orchestrator final-step status switch contains duplicated branch label, reducing precision in critical flow.
findings.suggestion: Remove the duplicate case and add an explicit exhaustive status assertion helper tied to shared status semantics so future enum/value drift is caught immediately.
evidence: In `checkOrchestratorCompletion`, switch over `finalStitchStatus` contains `case 'failed': case 'failed':` duplicate branch labels.
This branch handles terminal orchestration failure, so redundant cases indicate branch maintenance errors in critical state handling.

findings.summary: Per-shot serialized timeline queue can stall indefinitely on a hung task
findings.suggestion: Make timeout/abort enforcement part of the queue contract: add a required per-task timeout (or wrapper overload) in `runSerializedTimelineWrite`, emit a terminal queue event on timeout, and add tests for blocked-first-write + second-write recovery behavior.
evidence: `runSerializedTimelineWrite` waits on `previousTail` with no queue-level timeout or circuit breaker, so unresolved tasks block the shot queue indefinitely.
Timeouts are optional and caller-managed (`runTimelineWriteWithTimeout`), but queue entry does not require/verify timeout-wrapped tasks.
Existing tests cover ordering and timeout helper behavior, not queue recovery when a queued task never resolves.

findings.summary: complete_task request parsing diverges from shared edge parsing contract
findings.suggestion: Refactor `parseCompleteTaskRequest` to consume `parseJsonBodyStrict` + shared failure mapping from `edgeRequest.ts`, then update direct tests to assert the same envelope/error-code taxonomy used by other edge handlers.
evidence: `complete_task/request.ts` returns a custom `{ success: true/false }` parse result and `Response` directly, while shared edge utilities use `OperationResult` and centralized failure-response conversion.
The local parser uses `req.json()` and collapses malformed/non-object JSON into `invalid_json_body`, while shared strict parser emits shape-specific failures (`invalid_json_body_shape`).
Tests are split by implementation and currently validate both behaviors independently, reinforcing inconsistent API semantics between edge functions.

findings.summary: Task-ID parser marks empty-string task IDs as valid
findings.suggestion: Tighten parser contract to treat blank strings and all-blank arrays as `invalid_tasks_shape` (or a dedicated `invalid_task_id_value` status), propagate that status through repository/cache entries, and add explicit unit tests for blank/whitespace task IDs.
evidence: `parseGenerationTaskId` validates only runtime type (`string` or `string[]`) and returns status `ok` even for `''` values.
`getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` propagates parser status directly, so mappings can be marked `ok` while task IDs are unusable for downstream lookups/prefetch.
Multiple consumers rely on parser status for cache/task metadata health, so this silently understates data-quality failures.

findings.summary: Existing-generation lookup swallows non-not-found errors and lacks duplicate-row handling tests
findings.suggestion: Change lookup to deterministic retrieval (`order + limit(1) + maybeSingle` or explicit list query), treat multi-match as a typed degraded error instead of `null`, and add tests for duplicate-hit scenarios to prevent silent duplicate creation paths.
evidence: `findExistingGeneration` uses `.single()` and returns `null` for any error except `PGRST116`, which includes multi-row or query-shape failures.
Returning `null` on duplicate-match errors causes callers to proceed as if no generation exists, risking duplicate creation.
Current tests assert this fallback-to-null behavior for generic errors but do not cover multi-row ambiguity or remediation behavior.

findings.summary: Structure-video migration forcibly rewrites mode to `uni3c`, losing prior structure semantics.
findings.suggestion: Restrict migration coercion to true legacy single-field payloads only. Preserve existing `structure_videos[].structure_type` when present, and run an explicit one-time migration script if uni3c-only behavior is truly intended.
evidence: `migrateToArrayFormat` maps existing `structure_videos` and unconditionally sets `structure_type: 'uni3c'` (not only legacy single-field inputs).
`normalizeStructureGuidance` derives `target` and preprocessing from `structure_type`; forced rewrites change downstream guidance behavior.
The same hook persists migrated values back via `saveToDatabase`, so the rewrite is durable, not transient.

findings.summary: Deprecated structure-video fields remain embedded in core UI state contracts.
findings.suggestion: Split contracts into `CanonicalStructureVideo` (UI/runtime) and `LegacyStructureVideoAdapterInput` (boundary-only). Remove legacy field inheritance from UI types and centralize compatibility mapping inside dedicated adapters.
evidence: `StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` extends `LegacyStructureVideoFields` directly, despite comments saying canonical contracts are legacy-free.
`useStructureVideo` reads/writes `motion_strength`, `structure_type`, `uni3c_end_percent` in normal state/persistence flow, not only adapter boundaries.
This keeps deprecated fields alive in day-to-day editor state and delays the stated removal gate in `legacyStructureVideo.ts`.

findings.summary: Helper writes transport snake_case keys into domain `GenerationRow` shape.
findings.suggestion: Return domain-consistent keys (`createdAt`) from `variantToGenerationRow` and keep `created_at` handling strictly in explicit DTO mappers at API/DB boundaries.
evidence: `variantToGenerationRow` returns `created_at` while `PersistedGenerationRow` defines `createdAt`.
`GenerationRowLegacyAliases` documents `created_at` as transport alias on DTO boundary, but helper emits it directly in domain row creation.
This mixes DTO and domain contracts in one helper and makes consumers depend on mixed naming conventions.

findings.summary: Documentation-heavy scaffolding obscures real invariants and drifts from behavior.
findings.suggestion: Prune comment scaffolding to contract-critical notes only, move migration status to tracked ADR/deprecation policy docs, and prefer executable assertions/tests over prose for invariants.
evidence: Seed files include high comment-density modules with minimal logic (multiple index/barrel files mostly explanatory comments).
Narrative comments about migration cleanliness coexist with active legacy-field coupling in adjacent runtime code, reducing trust in comments as source of truth.
Large comment banners and section dividers add maintenance overhead but do not enforce or validate constraints.

findings.summary: Top-level `shared` directories act as catch-all buckets instead of domain boundaries
findings.suggestion: Run a staged reorg: Stage 1 create target folders (`src/features/resources/components`, `src/features/shots/components`, `src/features/lightbox/hooks`, `src/shared/hooks/tasks`, `src/shared/services/resources`). Stage 2 move domain files first (`DatasetBrowserModal`, `CreateShotModal`, `useLightboxOpen`, `generationTaskBridge`) into those folders without behavior changes. Stage 3 update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/components/DatasetBrowserModal|@/shared/components/CreateShotModal|@/shared/hooks/useLightboxOpen|@/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge" src` then apply path rewrites. Stage 4 validate with `npm run typecheck && npm run test -- DatasetBrowserModal CreateShotModal useGenerationTaskDetails`.
evidence: `src/shared/components/` has 50 production files in structure profile and 61 top-level files on disk, including both generic UI and domain-specific modals.
`src/shared/hooks/` and `src/shared/lib/` are similarly flat at root (69/67 production files), making ownership discovery depend on file-name guessing rather than folder boundaries.
Cross-folder coupling is high from these roots (`components -> ui` 84, `components -> hooks` 41, `hooks -> lib` 51), consistent with umbrella-folder organization rather than bounded modules.

findings.summary: `shared/lib` contains React Query hook code, blurring utility vs runtime layer boundaries
findings.suggestion: Staged move: Stage 1 create `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePrimaryTaskMapping.ts` and move the hook there; keep a temporary re-export shim in old path. Stage 2 keep repository lookup (`getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`) in `generationTaskRepository.ts` only. Stage 3 update imports via `rg -l "@/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge" src` and rewrite to new hook path. Stage 4 remove shim after green checks with `npm run typecheck && npm run test -- useGenerationTaskDetails generationTaskBridge`.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.ts` imports `useMutation` and exports `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`, but lives under `shared/lib` where most files are non-React utility/repository code.
`useGenerationTaskDetails` consumes this hook from a `lib` path, forcing React runtime semantics to appear as a library concern.
The same file includes an `async` wrapper (`getPrimaryTaskMapping`) around direct repository forwarding, adding extra indirection in a mislayered module.

findings.summary: A single shared modal owns two resource domains plus data orchestration and auth access
findings.suggestion: Staged split: Stage 1 extract `useResourceBrowserData` and `ResourceBrowserGrid` into `src/features/resources/`. Stage 2 add thin feature adapters (`StyleReferenceBrowserModal`, `StructureVideoBrowserModal`) in each feature folder with mode-specific contracts. Stage 3 keep `DatasetBrowserModal` as temporary compatibility wrapper delegating to adapters. Stage 4 migrate importers (`ReferenceSection`, `StructureVideoSection`, timeline/batch guidance) and validate with `npm run typecheck && npm run test -- DatasetBrowserModal StructureVideoSection ReferenceSection`.
evidence: `DatasetBrowserModal` contains item renderers, search/pagination state, ownership visibility toggles, Supabase session loading, and `useList*`/`useUpdateResource` orchestration in one unit.
The same component handles both `style-reference` and `structure-video` modes with divergent metadata contracts and selection flows.
It is consumed by unrelated feature surfaces (image reference selection and timeline structure-video selection), so feature-specific policy and UX decisions are centralized in a generic shared modal.

findings.summary: Lightbox open-state is propagated through a global CSS class contract instead of explicit state
findings.suggestion: Staged replacement: Stage 1 introduce `LightboxStateContext` in `src/features/lightbox/state/` and update lightbox open/close flows to publish explicit state. Stage 2 make `useBottomOffset` consume the typed context while keeping CSS class toggling as compatibility side-effect. Stage 3 remove DOM class observer hook and relocate residual style-only logic to lightbox feature. Stage 4 update imports with `rg -l "useLightboxOpen|lightbox-open" src` and validate via `npm run typecheck && npm run test -- useBottomOffset`.
evidence: `useLightboxOpen` derives state by observing `document.documentElement.classList.contains('lightbox-open')`.
`viewportLockService` mutates that class directly, and layout behavior (`useBottomOffset`) depends on it, creating implicit coupling through DOM/global styles.
This coupling is cross-boundary (service -> CSS -> hook -> layout feature) and not represented by an explicit typed interface.

findings.summary: Supabase access is coupled to hidden bootstrap ordering via mutable module state and throw-on-access semantics.
findings.suggestion: Replace throw-based global readiness with an explicit runtime provider/registry interface that is injected into repositories/hooks; for compatibility, keep `getSupabaseClientResult()` and migrate call sites to branch on readiness instead of relying on global bootstrap order.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` throws unless `initializeSupabase()` has already flipped `hasInitializedRuntimeClient` (`client.ts:11-47`).
Bootstrap performs that init in `initializeAppEnvironment()` (`bootstrap.tsx:84-86`), so callers outside that path can fail at runtime.
Repositories directly call `getSupabaseClient()` (`generationTaskRepository.ts:1,77,81`) and therefore inherit this implicit init contract.

findings.summary: Debug and runtime managers are exposed through mutable `window` globals, increasing cross-module hidden coupling.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate debug exposure behind one typed `debugRegistry` module with explicit `register/get` APIs and teardown support; keep `window` writes in a single boundary file to avoid ad-hoc globals and reduce hidden startup dependencies.
evidence: Supabase debug init writes `window.__supabase_client__` and `window.supabase` (`initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals.ts:29-30`).
Reconnect scheduler uses a global singleton and also writes `window.__RECONNECT_SCHEDULER__` in dev (`ReconnectScheduler.ts:147-161`).
Data freshness manager exports a process singleton and writes `window.__DATA_FRESHNESS_MANAGER__` via bootstrap-loaded registration (`DataFreshnessManager.ts:360-369`, `bootstrap.tsx:99-102`).

findings.summary: Scroll-state effect creates timers that are never cleared because cleanup is returned from the event handler instead of the effect.
findings.suggestion: Store timer id in a ref and clear/restart it inside `handleScroll`; clear it in effect cleanup alongside removing the listener.
evidence: In `useClipManager`, `handleScroll` returns `() => clearTimeout(timer)` (`useClipManager.ts:304-307`), but DOM listeners ignore returned values from handlers.
Effect cleanup only removes the listener (`useClipManager.ts:309-310`), so rapid scroll events queue many outstanding timers and delayed state updates.
`JoinClipsPage` depends on `isScrolling` for drag/drop behavior, so timer churn directly affects UI interaction consistency.

findings.summary: Image lightbox state composition is over-fragmented into pass-through builders/facades, increasing indirection without reducing responsibility.
findings.suggestion: Refactor around explicit domain controllers (navigation, variants, shot actions, edit mode) with narrow contracts and direct ownership boundaries; remove pass-through slice builders where no translation/validation occurs.
evidence: `ImageLightbox` creates many slice-builder wrappers (`buildImageShared*`) that mostly remap props one-to-one (`ImageLightbox.tsx:199-320`).

`useSharedLightboxState` then re-wraps these into additional sub-facades (`useSharedVariantsState`, `useLightboxNavigationModel`, `useLightboxShotActions`) with broad composite contracts (`useSharedLightboxState.ts:304-470`).

The same ‘mega component + adapter layers’ pattern appears in `MediaGalleryItem` (700+ LOC hotspot with many mixed concerns and hook orchestration), matching the packet’s signal-density/complexity concentration.

findings.summary: Completion flow acknowledges success after core task update even when orchestrator/billing follow-ups fail, creating workflow integrity blind spots.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit follow-up state fields (e.g., `post_completion_status`) and guarded retry queueing for orchestrator/billing actions; return success with degraded status only when retries are scheduled, not when failures are dropped.
evidence: Task is set to `Complete` before orchestration and billing follow-ups (`index.ts:383-399`).
Orchestrator completion failures are logged and swallowed (`index.ts:402-421`), then response still returns success (`index.ts:439-455`).

Billing follow-up failures are also warning-only (`index.ts:423-437`), so pipeline can report completed while downstream state remains stale, encouraging repeated follow-up churn without a gating/retry contract.

findings.summary: Supabase legacy-compat architecture is governed by conflicting policy definitions
findings.suggestion: Converge to one canonical migration contract. Stage 1: choose canonical alias/specifier in `legacySupabasePolicy.ts` and update `check-legacy-supabase-usage.mjs` to consume policy constants instead of hardcoded values. Stage 2: align `legacySupabaseFacade.contract.test.ts` with the same specifier and budget source. Stage 3: run `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage && npm run test:contracts && npm run test:arch` and fail PRs on divergence.
evidence: `legacySupabasePolicy.ts` sets specifier to `@/integrations/supabase/client` and budgets [160, 96, 64, 32], but `check-legacy-supabase-usage.mjs` enforces `@/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade` and budgets [129, 96, 64, 32].
Contract tests validate one migration path while CI script enforces another, creating incompatible boundary contracts.

findings.summary: Governance contract docs no longer describe actual runtime contract surfaces
findings.suggestion: Treat governance docs as executable contract artifacts: update docs to match current entrypoints and symbols, then add a doc-contract verifier that checks referenced file paths and exported symbols. Wire it into `npm run check:contracts` so docs drift fails the same gate as code-contract drift.
evidence: Supabase contract doc says bootstrap calls `initializeSupabaseClientRuntime()` via `initRuntimes.ts`, but runtime bootstrap calls `initializeSupabase()` in `app/bootstrap.tsx`.
Supabase doc references `hasInitializedSupabaseClientRuntime()` accessor not present in `client.ts`.
Error-handling doc points to non-existent `runtimeErrorCore.ts` / `normalizeAndReportError`; actual core API is `reportError` in `coreReporter.ts`.

findings.summary: Quality gates for contract governance are failing against current production tree
findings.suggestion: Restore gate integrity in three steps: 1) decide whether `getSupabaseClientResult` is public contract and either add it to `config/contracts/registry.json` or make it internal; 2) add required `@uiContract` tag to `src/shared/components/ui/contracts/button.tsx`; 3) enforce a CI pre-merge job running `npm run quality:check` with no allowed skips.
evidence: Running `node scripts/quality/check-contract-governance.mjs` fails: unclassified `getSupabaseClientResult` export in `client.ts` and missing required `@uiContract` tag in `contracts/button.tsx`.
This indicates governance metadata and contract source files are not synchronized, reducing trust in quality-gate health.

findings.summary: Generation/shot type ownership is split across canonical and legacy packages
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) move all generation/shot consumers to `@/domains/generation/types` (start with `useGenerationMutations.ts`, `VideoShotDisplay.tsx`); 2) keep `src/types/generationAndShots.ts|generationRow.ts|shot.ts` as temporary no-import deprecation stubs; 3) remove `@/types/shots` alias path entirely; 4) validate with `node scripts/quality/check-shots-shim-usage.mjs --max 0`, `npm run test:contracts`, and `npm run typecheck:strict-probe` before deleting residual shims.
evidence: Canonical domain type barrel exists at `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts`, but compatibility surface in `src/types/*` remains actively referenced.
Two production modules still import `@/types/shots`, and the zero-budget gate fails (`[shots-shim-check] FAILED: found 2 imports ... max allowed: 0`).
Directory-profile evidence shows both directories are active (`src/domains/generation/types/` avg fan-in 25.3; `src/types/` avg fan-in 6.3), indicating unresolved package boundary transition.

findings.summary: Two active workflow-control implementations duplicate shot-association behavior and split one responsibility across parallel UI paths.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into one workflow-controls domain component with layout-specific visual wrappers only; centralize shot-association action state and async flow in a single hook/model consumed by all layouts.
evidence: `LightboxLayout` renders both control systems depending on layout path (`<WorkflowControlsBar {...props.workflowBar} />` and `<WorkflowControls {...workflowControlsProps} />`).
`WorkflowControlsBar` and `WorkflowControls` both own nearly identical concerns (shot selection, add-with/without-position, apply-settings) and pass many of the same fields.
`ShotSelectorControls` adds another overlapping layer, including its own asynchronous add-variant/add-without-position flows and optimistic UI signaling.

findings.summary: Lightbox layers add indirection without transformation, inflating interfaces and obscuring ownership.
findings.suggestion: Replace pass-through layers with a smaller, behavior-oriented lightbox context/model (commands + derived state), and remove no-op presenter wrappers; keep mapping only at true boundary translations.
evidence: `MediaGalleryLightbox` decomposes a large `session` object into sub-contracts, then reconstructs another large prop object for `MediaLightbox`.
`MediaLightbox` groups flat props via `useBundledLightboxProps` then dispatches them to `ImageLightbox`/`VideoLightbox`; this is mostly reshaping, not behavior isolation.
`imageLightboxModels.ts` contains no-op wrappers (`buildWorkflowBarModel` and `buildWorkflowControlsModel` both `return input`), indicating ceremony without abstraction leverage.

findings.summary: Supabase client availability depends on two mutable init gates, creating hidden ordering coupling.
findings.suggestion: Unify readiness state into one source of truth (runtime module), make `getSupabaseClientResult()` derive from actual runtime client presence, and deprecate/remove the duplicate boolean gate.
evidence: `runtime/supabaseRuntime.ts` tracks singleton readiness via `runtimeClient`.
`client.ts` separately tracks readiness via `hasInitializedRuntimeClient` and blocks `getSupabaseClientResult()` when false.
Main bootstrap currently calls `initializeSupabase()`, but correctness depends on that exact path rather than a single authoritative runtime registry.

findings.summary: User-scope settings writes silently no-op when unauthenticated instead of surfacing auth failure
findings.suggestion: Make user-scope update fail closed: replace the `return null` branch with `throw new ToolSettingsError('auth_required', 'Authentication required')`, and keep mutation success paths strictly for persisted writes.
evidence: In `useToolSettings` mutationFn, `scope === 'user'` with no user ID returns `null` rather than throwing.
`handleMutationSuccess` explicitly treats `null` as successful early-exit, so callers see no failure signal.
The same subsystem's fetch path throws/returns `auth_required` when no user, so read/write auth contracts diverge.

findings.summary: Batch payload builder narrows reference-image contract and rewrites subject reference semantics
findings.suggestion: Align builder behavior to shared contract: emit reference fields based on explicit provided values (not only style-image presence), and pass through `subject_reference_image` when supplied instead of forcing it to style image.
evidence: Shared task contract allows `subject_reference_image` independent of `style_reference_image`.
`buildBatchTaskParams` always sets `subject_reference_image` to the style image and only emits all reference fields when style image exists.
This hidden narrowing is not represented in the shared type contract, so cross-module behavior diverges from declared API flexibility.

findings.summary: Prompt-count loader can return NaN from malformed session storage without fallback
findings.suggestion: Clamp helper output to a finite positive integer (`const n = Number.parseInt(...); return Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 ? n : 1;`) and keep all call sites unchanged.
evidence: `getStoredPromptCount` returns `parseInt(stored, 10)` directly; `parseInt('bad', 10)` yields `NaN`.
`usePromptManagement` uses this value as initial state for `lastKnownPromptCount`.
`usePromptPersistenceEffects` re-reads the same helper and writes derived counts, so invalid numeric state can persist until overwritten.

findings.summary: Delete confirmation flow swallows mutation failures without any reporting path
findings.suggestion: Return an explicit result from `confirmDelete` (e.g., `{ ok: false, error }`) and report via `normalizeAndPresentError` with `showToast: false`; let callers decide UX while preserving telemetry.
evidence: `confirmDelete` catches errors with `catch {}` and keeps dialog open, but never calls `normalizeAndPresentError` or returns failure metadata.
Upstream consumers only receive pending/deleting state, so operational failures are invisible unless mutation internals toast independently.

findings.summary: Billing hook uses generic thrown strings while sibling hooks use normalized app errors
findings.suggestion: Standardize all Supabase-facing hooks on one error contract (prefer normalized AppError with preserved cause) and remove generic re-stringification in billing fetchers.
evidence: `useCredits` throws new `Error('Failed to fetch ...')` from Supabase failures, discarding structured original error properties.
`useApiTokens` and other hooks consistently use `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`, creating divergent error payloads and handling semantics across adjacent features.

findings.summary: Generation params mapper asserts complex payload types without structural validation
findings.suggestion: Introduce field-level validators/type guards for `OrchestratorDetailsPayload` and `GenerationExtraPayload`, and surface validation failures through structured mapper diagnostics.
evidence: `toOrchestratorDetailsPayload` and `toGenerationExtraPayload` cast any record to typed payload interfaces after only `isRecord` check.
Typed outputs can include malformed nested fields while compile-time contracts imply validated structure.

findings.summary: Image-edit API is split into thin relays that add indirection without behavior
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one canonical boundary: keep either `features/image-edit` or `domains/imageEdit` as public facade and route imports directly to it; remove redundant pass-through modules.
evidence: Feature and domain layers mostly re-export shared modules without translation, policy, or invariants.
Callers traverse multiple package surfaces to reach effectively one implementation in `shared`.

findings.summary: Global Supabase singleton requires strict bootstrap order across many hooks
findings.suggestion: Move Supabase access behind a React runtime provider/context (or injected accessor) initialized once at app root; make hooks depend on context rather than process-global mutable state.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient` throws unless `initializeSupabase()` has already run, enforced via global mutable flags/singletons.
Hooks across app/features call `getSupabaseClient()` directly, coupling normal execution to one startup sequence.

findings.summary: Instrumentation tests verify exports but not behavioral patching or failure paths
findings.suggestion: Add behavior tests that simulate window/websocket/realtime objects, assert patch idempotency, event capture, and error-path handling; keep export smoke tests as a thin sanity layer only.
evidence: Both direct tests only assert function existence (`toBeDefined`) and do not execute patchers or corruption-event collection paths.
Production behavior includes monkey-patching globals and object properties, which is high risk without behavior-level tests.

findings.summary: Generation transport still carries parallel legacy aliases, extending migration debt
findings.suggestion: Define a migration cutoff release: stage 1 deprecate legacy aliases via runtime warnings in mapper layer, stage 2 remove alias fields from DTO/public exports, stage 3 run repo-wide compile/test to update remaining callsites.
evidence: `GenerationRowLegacyAliases` includes both snake_case and legacy camelCase IDs (`shot_generation_id`, `shotImageEntryId`) in active type surface.
Mapper/type layers continue to support dual naming schemes, increasing ongoing maintenance and ambiguity.

findings.summary: Image-edit capability is split across thin directories with minimal local responsibility
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) choose target folder (`src/features/image-edit` as canonical surface); 2) move/re-export `shared/imageEditCore` symbols into that folder in one direction only; 3) remove `src/domains/imageEdit` wrappers; 4) update imports with `rg -l "@/domains/imageEdit|@/shared/imageEditCore|@/features/image-edit" src | xargs sed` (or codemod); 5) validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm -s vitest run`.
evidence: Directory profile metrics show `src/features/image-edit/` (6 files, ~85 LOC) and `src/domains/imageEdit/` (2 files, ~25 LOC), both largely pass-through.
Primary implementation logic remains in `src/shared/imageEditCore/*`, leaving intermediary folders as indirection islands.
